name: Nextcloud MCP Server
what_it_does: Community MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with self-hosted Nextcloud instances — accessing files, managing directories, reading documents, and navigating Nextcloud storage. Enables AI assistants to integrate with self-hosted Nextcloud for document management, file organization, and cloud storage workflows.
use_cases: Reading and managing files in self-hosted Nextcloud from document management agents
Organizing Nextcloud directories and searching for documents from file management agents
Integrating Nextcloud storage into AI document processing workflows
Accessing Nextcloud shared files and collaborative spaces from collaboration agents
not_for: Nextcloud Cloud (nextcloud.com hosted) — designed for self-hosted instances
Teams using other cloud storage (Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive — use their specific MCPs)
Nextcloud without WebDAV or API access enabled
best_when: A team running self-hosted Nextcloud for privacy-first file storage wants AI agents to access and manage documents — integrating Nextcloud into document workflows without relying on third-party cloud storage providers.
avoid_when: You use a hosted cloud storage provider (Google Drive, Dropbox, etc.) or don't have Nextcloud deployed. Self-hosted Nextcloud setup is required.

auth.notes: Nextcloud app password or API token recommended. Basic auth with Nextcloud credentials. Use app-specific passwords rather than main account password.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — Nextcloud is open source; infrastructure costs apply
pricing.paid_tiers: None for software; infrastructure hosting costs apply
pricing.notes: Nextcloud is free open source (AGPL). Infrastructure costs for self-hosting. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by your Nextcloud instance capacity and network

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Self-hosted files contain personal/business data — handle with appropriate data governance
Use Nextcloud app passwords instead of main account credentials for better security isolation
Nextcloud version compatibility varies — test against your specific Nextcloud version
TLS setup quality depends on your self-hosted deployment — ensure HTTPS is properly configured
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Self-hosted file system access. Use app-specific passwords. Ensure TLS/HTTPS on your Nextcloud instance. File access limited to authenticated user's permissions.

security_score_components.security_notes: File system access. App passwords for credential isolation. TLS quality depends on your deployment. PRIVACY: business file content.
